req.title: Create patient (boundary)
req.statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-026 mandates that a clinician can create a Patient resource with valid demographics (positive path), that missing mandatory elements are rejected with HTTP 422 (negative path), and that boundary values on timestamps and numeric fields are exercised (boundary path). EP covers the valid-input partition; NEG covers the missing-field partition; BVA covers edge timestamps (epoch zero, far-future date) and numeric extremes (e.g. age-related birthDate). A fourth DT case is added to ensure the decision logic for multiple missing fields is covered, and a fifth STC case verifies that the resource moves from non-existent to stored state correctly.

tests.name: test_create_patient_valid_demographics
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Patient with the test identifier does not already exist
actions: Build a minimal valid FHIR Patient payload with resourceType, name, gender, and birthDate
POST /Patient with Content-Type application/fhir+json and Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a server-assigned id
trace: R-HLT-026: patient resource stored on valid demographics
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_valid_demographics(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_id}],
        "name": [{"family": "Smith", "given": ["Alice"]}],
        "gender": "female",
        "birthDate": "1990-06-15"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert "id" in body
    <fixture: cleanup_patient(body["id"])>

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_mandatory_name
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a Patient payload that intentionally omits the mandatory 'name' element
POST /Patient with Content-Type application/fhir+json and Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message referencing the missing field
trace: R-HLT-026: creation with missing mandatory element shall be rejected
NEG: invalid-input equivalence class — mandatory field absent
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_missing_mandatory_name(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_id}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1985-03-22"
        # 'name' intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    body_str = str(body).lower()
    assert "name" in body_str or "mandatory" in body_str or "required" in body_str

tests.name: test_create_patient_birthdate_epoch_zero_boundary
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
System under test defines 1900-01-01 as the minimum allowed birthDate
actions: Build a Patient payload with birthDate set to '1900-01-01' (lower boundary)
POST /Patient with Content-Type application/fhir+json and Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 201 Created (boundary value is within acceptable range)
Response body contains resourceType = Patient with the supplied birthDate preserved
trace: R-HLT-026: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
BVA: minimum allowable birthDate boundary value
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_birthdate_epoch_zero_boundary(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_id}],
        "name": [{"family": "Boundary", "given": ["Min"]}],
        "gender": "unknown",
        "birthDate": "1900-01-01"  # lower timestamp boundary
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("birthDate") == "1900-01-01"
    <fixture: cleanup_patient(body["id"])>

tests.name: test_create_patient_birthdate_future_beyond_today_rejected
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
System under test rejects birthDate strictly after today's date
actions: Compute a date one day beyond today dynamically
Build a Patient payload with that future birthDate
POST /Patient with Content-Type application/fhir+json and Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body indicates birthDate is invalid or in the future
trace: R-HLT-026: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
BVA: upper timestamp boundary — birthDate one day past today
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_create_patient_birthdate_future_beyond_today_rejected(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_id):
    future_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_id}],
        "name": [{"family": "Future", "given": ["Pat"]}],
        "gender": "other",
        "birthDate": future_date
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, resp.text
    body_str = str(resp.json()).lower()
    assert "birthdate" in body_str or "future" in body_str or "invalid" in body_str

tests.name: test_create_patient_multiple_missing_mandatory_fields
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a Patient payload that omits BOTH 'name' AND 'birthDate'
POST /Patient with Content-Type application/fhir+json and Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body reports at least one validation error covering the missing fields
trace: R-HLT-026: creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected
DT: decision rule — both mandatory fields absent triggers 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_multiple_missing_mandatory_fields(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_id}],
        "gender": "female"
        # 'name' and 'birthDate' intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body  # non-empty error body

tests.name: test_create_patient_state_transitions_nonexistent_to_stored
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
No Patient resource with the test identifier exists (pre-state: non-existent)
actions: Verify via GET /Patient?identifier=<unique_id> that the resource does not exist (0 results)
POST /Patient with a fully valid payload
Verify via GET /Patient/<id> that the resource now exists (post-state: stored)
expected: Initial GET returns empty bundle (0 entries)
Subsequent GET /Patient/<id> returns HTTP 200 with matching resourceType
trace: R-HLT-026: patient resource stored after creation
STC: state transition non-existent → stored verified via read-back
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_state_transitions_nonexistent_to_stored(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, unique_id):
    search_url = f"{base_url}/Patient?identifier=urn:test|{unique_id}"
    pre = requests.get(search_url, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json().get("total", 0) == 0, "Pre-state: resource must not exist"

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_id}],
        "name": [{"family": "Transition", "given": ["Test"]}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "2000-01-01"
    }
    create = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert create.status_code == 201, create.text
    patient_id = create.json()["id"]

    read = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert read.status_code == 200
    assert read.json().get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    <fixture: cleanup_patient(patient_id)>
